gloss:
Statement of record: `Eidometry.Thesis.counterfactual_ct3b_repair` (Eidometry thesis, section Counterfactuals).

The repaired version of the disproved claim: once you assume a measurable selector that respects the "behaves the same" relation, its fixed-point set is measurable and every state has exactly one behaviorally-equivalent fixed point. This is the surviving, usable form of the conjecture.

Discharged by [MTH.R-2026-1210].

statement.pretty: ∀ {S : Type} [inst : MeasurableSpace S] [StandardBorelSpace S] (T : ℕ → S → ℝ) (f σ : S → S), Measurable σ → (∀ (x : S), (TLT.NonIdentifiability.bisimilarity T (TLT.NonIdentifiability.pureKernel f)) (σ x) x) → (∀ (x y : S), (TLT.NonIdentifiability.bisimilarity T (TLT.NonIdentifiability.pureKernel f)) x y → σ x = σ y) → MeasurableSet {x | σ x = x} ∧ ∀ (x : S), ∃! y, y ∈ {z | σ z = z} ∧ (TLT.NonIdentifiability.bisimilarity T (TLT.NonIdentifiability.pureKernel f)) x y

title: Eidometry.Thesis.counterfactual_ct3b_repair
